title: Largest ever deployment of RAF Poseidon Maritime Aircraft supporting NATO in Iceland

sections.heading: Deployment
sections.content: Three P-8A Poseidon aircraft from 120 Squadron at RAF Lossiemouth deployed to Keflavik Air Base in Iceland in November 2025, marking the largest RAF Poseidon deployment so far. The Royal Air Force said the aircraft and associated personnel were carrying out routine operations in the region and underlining the UK's commitment to NATO.

sections.heading: Operational training
sections.content: The deployment also gave air and ground crews an opportunity to practise Agile Combat Employment functions, designed to show that the RAF can deploy and use its capabilities quickly in demanding environments. The RAF said Iceland holds a critical geo-strategic position in NATO planning because of its role in monitoring and securing the High North, the North Atlantic and the Arctic region.

sections.heading: NATO context
sections.content: The mission supported NATO's Peacetime Vigilance Activity under NATO Maritime Command and aligned with the Supreme Allied Commander Europe's strategic direction. While in Iceland, the RAF worked with US and Canadian partners. The service also noted 120 Squadron's historical ties to Iceland, where it was based during the Second World War.
